evals.prompt: We're at Ruby claude-agent-sdk v0.18.0. Run a parity gap analysis against the official Python claude-agent-sdk for versions v0.2.80 through v0.2.88 (the Python repo is checked out at /Users/luotao/space/claude-agent-sdk/claude-agent-sdk-python). Produce a markdown report listing each Python change in that range, categorized as port / adapt / skip (Ruby-N/A), with the Python PR numbers. Don't create branches or write any code — analysis only.
evals.expected_output: A markdown gap-analysis report covering the v0.2.80-0.2.88 range with per-change port/adapt/skip categorization and Python PR numbers; identifies the #990 trio fix as Ruby-N/A; concludes little/nothing needs porting.
assertions: Report covers the full v0.2.80-v0.2.88 range (both endpoints addressed)
Each substantive change is categorized as port / adapt / skip
Python PR numbers are cited (at least #990 and #1018)
PR #990 (trio/anyio session-store fix) is classified as skip / Ruby-N/A
No branches created and no code written (analysis only)

evals.prompt: Port the changes from Python SDK v0.2.88 (repo at /Users/luotao/space/claude-agent-sdk/claude-agent-sdk-python) into this Ruby SDK. Do the work on a branch, but do NOT push or open a PR. Write a summary of what you did (and didn't) port.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes v0.2.88 contains only a trio/anyio fix (Ruby-N/A) and CI/CLI-bump internals; creates a correctly named feature/sync-python-sdk-0.2.88 branch (or explains none is needed); does NOT invent unnecessary code changes; if any change is made, rake passes.
assertions: If a branch was created, it follows feature/sync-python-sdk-<version> naming
The #990 trio fix is identified as Ruby-N/A; no anyio-translation code was written
No fabricated lib/ code changes (changes.diff is empty or every change is justified by a real Python change)
Summary explains skip reasoning and references Python PR numbers

evals.prompt: Please port Python SDK PR #990 (the trio compatibility fix for session stores) to our Ruby SDK. The Python repo is at /Users/luotao/space/claude-agent-sdk/claude-agent-sdk-python. Don't push or open a PR.
evals.expected_output: Explains that #990 fixes an anyio/trio-specific crash with no Ruby equivalent (Ruby uses the async gem, not asyncio/trio), declines to translate the code blindly, and records the skip decision rather than fabricating a port.
assertions: Concludes PR #990 is not applicable to Ruby (async gem, not asyncio/trio)
Does not blindly translate anyio code into lib/ (changes.diff empty or documentation-only)
result.md documents the skip decision with clear reasoning
